title: Graph event (envelope)
description: One append-only event line in events.jsonl. The envelope is the durable unit of record. payload carries an optional node OR edge record (under payload.node / payload.edge) plus event-specific sibling fields; see provenance/event-taxonomy.md and provenance/graph-model.md. TOLERANT v0 draft: additionalProperties is permissive; the only hard constraints are the locked patterns and the tool.runtime enum.

event_id.description: Globally unique event identity. MUST be a ULID (see provenance/event-taxonomy.md). Not regex-enforced in v0.1 to keep the loose phase tolerant; the ULID requirement is normative in the docs.

artifact_id.description: The artifact this event belongs to. Under the single-writer assumption, seq is monotonic per artifact_id.

seq.description: Monotonic per-artifact order key. Under the documented single-writer assumption, seq is dense, gap-free, and equals creation order. A seq collision is a detectable integrity error (see provenance/event-taxonomy.md).

actor.description: Typed-prefix actor ID: human:, agent:, or service:. An agent acting for a person records the delegation in actor_detail.on_behalf_of.

actor_detail.description: Optional extension point for actor metadata and future signed-identity assertions.

on_behalf_of.description: Delegation: the human or service this actor is acting for.

event_type.description: Dotted type, e.g. claim.added. Validated by pattern here; the canonical registry lives in provenance/event-taxonomy.md and is enforced by the materializer (Phase 2), not by this schema.

payload.description: Event body. Record-creating events carry the node record under payload.node or the edge record under payload.edge, alongside event-specific sibling fields (reason, prior_status, superseded_id, detection_method, changed_fields, target_id, notes). Permissive object in v0.1.

tool.description: Optional runtime/tooling metadata for the event.

trace_id.description: W3C Trace Context trace-id: 16 bytes, lowercase hex. Correlation pointer into an OTel trace, not an identity. An all-zero value is invalid per W3C Trace Context (documented, not regex-enforced in v0.1).

span_id.description: W3C Trace Context span-id: 8 bytes, lowercase hex. An all-zero value is invalid per W3C Trace Context (documented, not regex-enforced in v0.1).
